headline: UAE to Leave OPEC After Nearly 60 Years
summary: The United Arab Emirates announced it will quit the OPEC oil cartel next month after nearly 60 years of membership. The move is seen as a blow to the group and its de facto leader Saudi Arabia, as global energy markets face supply crises.

headline: Trump Evacuated from White House Correspondents' Dinner After Security Incident
summary: President Trump and other high-ranking officials were abruptly evacuated from the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after a gunman charged a security checkpoint. The suspect had reportedly planned to target Trump administration officials.

headline: Ukraine Accuses Israel of Importing 'Stolen' Grain from Russia
summary: President Zelenskyy threatened sanctions against Israel, accusing it of importing grain that Russia allegedly stole from Ukraine. A vessel carrying the grain reportedly arrived at an Israeli port preparing to unload.

headline: King Charles Delivers Historic Address to US Congress
summary: King Charles became the first British monarch to address a joint session of the US Congress, speaking of the 'truly unique' relationship between the UK and US. He also attended a state dinner at the White House hosted by President Trump.

headline: Kim Jong Un Praises North Korean Troops Who 'Self-Blasted' in Ukraine
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un confirmed that soldiers fighting for Russia against Ukraine are being ordered to detonate grenades to kill themselves rather than be captured. He praised troops who 'self-blasted' at a memorial event in Pyongyang.

headline: James Comey Charged Over Instagram Post Allegedly Threatening Trump
summary: Former FBI Director James Comey has been charged over a 2025 Instagram post featuring a seashell photo that the Justice Department claims calls for violence against President Trump.

headline: France Urges Citizens to Leave Mali After Rebel Attacks
summary: France has urged its citizens to leave Mali following recent rebel attacks. The UK issued similar advice, warning citizens who remain that they do so at their own risk.

headline: Israeli 'Double-Tap' Strike Kills Rescue Workers in Lebanon
summary: An Israeli 'double-tap' strike killed three rescue workers in Lebanon, according to officials. Five people were reportedly killed in successive attacks on Tuesday.

headline: Afghanistan Women Footballers Can Return to Competition After FIFA Rule Changes
summary: FIFA has approved the return of Afghanistan's women's football team to international competition. Former captain Khalida Popal called the women a 'symbol of resilience.'

headline: Irish Government Announces Fuel Supports After Nationwide Protests
summary: The Irish government announced additional fuel supports for several sectors following nationwide protests in recent weeks over rising energy costs.
